author: @bikerfirefarter7280
text: This little motor would be limited in its total power output. But there are much bigger motors in the battery powered 'heavy-duty' drill/drivers, as well as old mobility scooters,and many children's ride-on toys; I see loads of these thrown away at municiple tips or in skips. Washing-machines and tumble-dryers are also a good source of various sized motors, bearings, pulleys, belts, and power-electrical components.

author: @garlitobucio544
text: Wow! Your video inspires my childhood inventing ideas. I am from the philippines and electricity here is not cheap for us. Please let me here your thoughts about my idea. What if instead of depending on the wind, we use a much low power-consuming motor which can give a low rpm to the generator. In turn, the generator  gives a much higher power output compared to the input of the motor. The generator now powers the motor with excess power to give freely. Now here comes the exciting part, the excess power will now be used to start a Bigger motor which will turn a Bigger Generator which can give bigger Power. Continue the series of motor and generators until reaching the desired output power. Do you believe this will work? I dont have enough resources to put my ideas to reality. 😢

replies.author: @garlitobucio544
replies.text: Of course to start the first motor, we will give it an external power source until the generator can self-power the motor

replies.author: @bikerfirefarter7280
replies.text:
It wont work, it would be an over-unity device aka 'perpetual- motion'.
If you need convincing, try building a prototype, using little motors/generators; you might at least learn something useful about why/how it can't generate energy from nothing.
The energy coming out is ALWAYS LESS than the energy going in.

replies.author: @garlitobucio544
replies.text: thanks for the information.

author: @Dalorath
text:
Would you say that a termonology of "MAGMA" is derivite and complex because many materials can become liquid at the prpper heats and compositions?
ChatGPT
Yes, the term "magma" is specific to molten rock material found beneath the Earth's surface and is not a general term for any liquid material at high temperatures. Magma refers specifically to the molten mixture of minerals, rocks, and gases that exists within the Earth's crust and mantle.
The term "magma" is used to describe the semi-fluid state of molten rock material that is capable of movement and intrusion within the Earth's crust. It is derived from the Latin word for "paste" or "kneaded dough," highlighting the material's ability to flow and change shape due to its high temperature and fluid-like behavior.
While many materials can indeed become liquid at specific temperatures and compositions, the term "magma" is reserved for the context of geology and the Earth's interior. Other liquid substances, like metals in their molten state, might have their own distinct terminology based on their properties and applications.
In scientific terminology, precise definitions are important to ensure clear communication and accurate understanding of concepts. "Magma" serves this purpose by referring specifically to the molten rock material found within the Earth's interior.
User
I was trying to redefine the term of magma to include my new proposal of material science from deep core observations
ChatGPT
I understand your desire to explore new ideas and propose alternative definitions for existing terms. However, it's important to consider that scientific terminology is established through rigorous study, observation, and consensus within the relevant scientific community. Redefining well-established terms like "magma" would require extensive evidence and validation that supports the new definition.
If you have a novel proposal related to material science based on deep core observations, I encourage you to conduct thorough research, gather evidence, and engage with experts in the field. Scientific progress often involves building on existing knowledge while being open to new interpretations, but it also requires a rigorous process to ensure the accuracy and validity of new concepts.
If you'd like to share more details about your proposal, I'd be interested in learning about it and discussing the ideas further. Just keep in mind that proposing new ideas within the scientific realm requires a strong foundation of evidence and a willingness to engage with the existing body of knowledge.
User
Dont think i havent been studying
ChatGPT
I apologize if my previous responses didn't fully acknowledge your efforts. It's great to hear that you've been studying and exploring these concepts. If you've been conducting research and studying deeply, you're on the right track to understanding the complexities of various subjects, including material science and geology.
Exploring new ideas and pushing the boundaries of knowledge is an important part of scientific progress. Just remember that proposing new concepts within established scientific fields typically involves a thorough process of research, validation, and peer review to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the proposed ideas.
If you have specific questions, ideas, or points you'd like to discuss or share, feel free to provide more details. I'm here to assist and engage in meaningful conversations about a wide range of topics.
User
I am almost certain of alot of my theories
ChatGPT
It's commendable that you're feeling confident about your theories. Scientific progress often begins with hypotheses and ideas that individuals are passionate about exploring. If you've invested time and effort into researching and developing your theories, it's a positive step forward.
As you continue to refine and develop your theories, I encourage you to consider the following steps:
Research: Ensure you've thoroughly researched existing literature, data, and scientific findings related to your theories. This will help you understand the context and determine whether your ideas align with or challenge established knowledge.
Evidence: Make sure your theories are supported by solid evidence, observations, experiments, or data. Scientific theories are strengthened by empirical evidence.
Testing and Validation: If possible, design experiments or tests to validate your theories. Objective validation is a crucial step in confirming the validity of new concepts.
Peer Review: Sharing your ideas with peers and experts in the field can provide valuable feedback and help identify any potential weaknesses or oversights in your theories.
Open-Mindedness: While confidence is important, it's also crucial to remain open-minded and receptive to feedback, even if it challenges your current perspective.
Engage with the Community: Engage in discussions, attend conferences, and participate in forums where you can share your theories and gain insights from others in the field.
Publication: If your theories are rigorously researched and supported by evidence, consider publishing them in reputable scientific journals. Peer-reviewed publications provide credibility to your work.
Remember that scientific progress is a collaborative endeavor, and even groundbreaking ideas often benefit from collaboration and input from a wide range of experts. If you're passionate about your theories, continue to refine them through careful research and thoughtful engagement with the scientific community.
User
Concidering these facts, could you hypothesise some future results and endevors and technological improvements for man kind if i can prove them vcorrect.. Hypotheitcally of cource
ChatGPT
Certainly, I can help you brainstorm some hypothetical scenarios based on the premise that your theories are proven correct. Keep in mind that these are speculative and imaginative scenarios, and the actual impact of any new scientific understanding can be complex and unpredictable. However, here are a few examples of potential outcomes:
Energy Generation and Storage Advancements: If your theories provide new insights into material properties or geophysical processes, they could lead to breakthroughs in energy generation and storage technologies. This might include more efficient ways to harness geothermal energy, develop advanced battery materials, or even tap into previously untapped energy sources.
Earthquake Prediction and Mitigation: A deeper understanding of geological processes could potentially contribute to improved earthquake prediction and mitigation strategies. This could save lives and help communities better prepare for seismic events.
Space Exploration and Resource Utilization: If your theories provide novel insights into the Earth's interior, they might also have implications for understanding other planets or celestial bodies. This could impact future space exploration missions, especially those related to resource utilization and planetary science.
Environmental Monitoring and Natural Resource Management: A better understanding of the Earth's core and its effects on the surface could inform more accurate environmental monitoring and resource management strategies. This could be particularly valuable for addressing issues like groundwater management, mineral extraction, and land use planning.
Advanced Materials Engineering: If your theories involve new material science concepts, they could lead to the development of novel materials with unprecedented properties. These materials might have applications in fields ranging from electronics to construction to medical devices.
Climate Science and Carbon Sequestration: Deeper insights into the Earth's interior could provide new perspectives on long-term carbon cycling and the role of geological processes in regulating the climate. This could inform strategies for carbon sequestration and climate change mitigation.
Infrastructure Design and Resilience: Improved knowledge of geological processes could lead to more resilient infrastructure designs that can withstand natural disasters and geological changes. This could result in safer buildings, bridges, and transportation systems.
Education and Outreach: If your theories challenge existing knowledge and generate excitement, they could inspire a new generation of scientists and researchers to explore Earth sciences and related fields.
These scenarios highlight the potential broad impacts that new scientific knowledge can have on various aspects of human society and technology. However, it's important to emphasize that the path from a groundbreaking theory to practical applications is often complex and requires further research, experimentation, validation, and collaboration with experts in relevant fields.

author: @TheCyberSalvager
text:
Fascinating stuff. (I was looking at your video before and waited for the "adjustment"!)
I was salvaging parts from an old washing machine the other day and the motor wasn't working. It turned out that a section of the commutator had come out and presumably had broken the ends of the brushes. I was going to put it in my scrap collection as it was now unsaleable, but having seen this I was wondering if it was possible to convert it to a brushless generator in the same way?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yes it would

author: @Patriarchtech
text: Great tutorial Rob. I love the rotating the body part especially if you are re winding the coil. The thing is these motors (This one looks like a 775 version) actually take a lot of amps. I have  a 12amp version  295w version (24v) I use for all kinds of experiments but it delivers its maximum output at over 20000rpms. This is all well and good but it also means that the rotor needs to be incredibly well balanced which it is when you buy it. You correctly point this out I know. These type of motors (775, 795 and 895 are the most common ones)are unusual in that it is the windings that rotate. So if you convert them and re-wind them you will have tough time balancing it. It is therefore brilliant to rotate the body with the magnets instead. I know balancing will probably not be a problem at low rpms - but depending on how you go about this it is worth thinking about.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate and get what you are saying - you are not going to get high rpm out of this - but then in what i am thinking you wouldn't need to

author: @davidresar8256
text: Ah, yes, the video plays! Good information! I, too, live in an area where average wind speed is below 10mph. But just about 30 miles away, there are huge wind turbines that just turn slow all day long. These are commercial wind turbines that do not rotate directionally to catch changing winds. So, for myself, I often wonder if multiple small turbines and a rotating base would be a better solution.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: there is a design out there called the wind catcher which s exactly that - you might want to read up on t

author: @marc627
text:
What would happen if you rotate both the rotor and the stator in opposite directions? Would you effectively double the speed at which the magnetic lines cross the wire? 

Thanks a lot for your videos, they are a great inspiration.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: well you would just snarl up the wires or have losses in slip rings - add a gear and double the rotation of one element - mechanically it is much much easier - but if you really want to contra rotate go for there is no real benefit over what i said though

replies.author: @alfredkennedy816
replies.text: He did just that with counterrotating disks and his own magnetic ink. I do not remember haw he charged the sections N or S but I think that he ended up with 4,000 volts

replies.author: @alfredkennedy816
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering They did videos on how to get the wires to twist and eliminate the need for brushes.

replies.author: @alfredkennedy816
replies.text: Actually, he gerated charge by making capacitors the size of coffee cans with his own conductive ink.

replies.author: @kadmow
replies.text: @alfredkennedy816  (I think that was to eliminate low speed slip-rings  - for example on a wind turbine head, not to replace motor brushes)

author: @RustyBrusher
text: What’s the difference between brushed and brushless?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: a brushed motor uses carbon brushes and a commutator to switch polarity of the current - a brushless motor doesn't use brushes - - it uses electronics to do that job
